what_it_does: Lists a batch of source files assigned to the claude-ds project for an interpretive pass, recording each file's project, relative path, and repository root.
observations: Contains six entries, all project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'.
Paths span app/ai/host-runtime.js, app/ai/host-serializer.js, app/app.css, app/App.jsx, app/canvases/AIStudio.jsx, and app/canvases/Architecture.jsx.
No symbols[] content is present; the file is purely a file-to-project allocation manifest.
The batch sits under wave-002/alloc, implying a wave-based allocation scheme.
standouts: Includes both runtime/serializer infrastructure files and two canvas components (AIStudio, Architecture).
Mixes JS, JSX, and CSS in one batch.
concerns: Absolute root path is tied to a specific home directory, which may limit portability.
No stated reason why these six files are grouped together in this batch.
notes: A wave-002 allocation manifest that registers six claude-ds source files for downstream interpretation.
questions: What selection criteria grouped these particular files into batch-003?
What interpretive operation is expected to run against this batch?
Should CSS files be interpreted alongside JS/JSX in the same batch?
intention_signals: Catalog a concrete slice of the claude-ds codebase so an interpretive/fusion step can operate on a known, bounded set of files in wave 002.
contribution: Provides a bounded, project-scoped file list that a fused system can use as an input set for interpretation, preserving the original grouping and root context.
summary_for_embedding: A wave-002 allocation batch JSON listing six claude-ds project files across app/ai, app root, and app/canvases with their common repository root.
intention_for_embedding: To register a specific batch of claude-ds source files for an interpretive or fusion pass during build preparation.
